_comment: Event schema for corbettclaims.com, consumed by
This file exists so the tooling never falls back to its BUILT-IN default.
The default happens to be this site's schema, so omitting --schema would
have worked by coincidence — and coincidence is the thing that breaks when
the default later changes or another site is onboarded. Pass it explicitly.
THIS FILE AND THE SCHEMA OBJECT IN analytics.js MUST AGREE. They are two
halves of one contract: analytics.js decides what the site is allowed to
PUBLISH, this decides what GTM is configured to READ. Both directions fail
closed — a key here the site never pushes is empty in GA4, a key the site
pushes that is missing here never arrives — so neither leaks, but both are
NEVER add a field read from secureFields beyond the three enums below.
That object holds the claimant, insured and owner names, the owner address,
the claim number, date of loss, email, phone and the free-text message.
